findings.description: Outdated versions of Flask (2.3.3) and Requests (2.31.0) contain known CVEs in production.
findings.exploit_scenario: Attacker analyzes versions running externally or via error strings. Depending on the exact CVE, they trigger payload structures handling requests (e.g. request parsing logic in Flask or requests SSRF redirects) to exploit known CVE-2026-27205 or CVE-2024-35195.
findings.impact: Potential system compromise, dependency manipulation, or unauthorized request executions depending on the CVE details.
findings.recommendation: Update to Flask-3.1.3 and requests-2.32.4 inside requirements.txt.

findings.description: User input is directly concatenated into the instruction prompt string passed to the Gemini model, causing prompt injection.
findings.impact: Integrity violation of the analysis, manipulation of return values to cause denial of analysis or misleading outputs on the UI. While no backend server RCE or data breach is possible here, it breaks entirely the core business logic capability.
findings.recommendation: Separate the instructions from the user data. Put the instructions into the `system_instruction` argument when calling GenerativeModel, and only pass `{text}` in `generate_content()`.
